explanation: Yogurt powder is dried fermented dairy providing lactic acid, proteins, amino acids, and probiotic metabolites with mild exfoliating (AHA), conditioning, and barrier-supporting properties from Lactobacillus fermentation. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations and used in conditioning and brightening formulas for its natural lactic acid and nutrient content; individuals with dairy allergy should note its milk-protein origin.

The asterisk indicates organic certification; Yucca brevifolia (Joshua tree) root extract provides steroidal saponins with natural surfactant, anti-inflammatory, and skin-conditioning properties. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic extract concentrations and used in natural cleansing and soothing formulas for its saponin and antioxidant content.

Zanthoxylum piperitum (Sichuan pepper) fruit extract provides hydroxy-alpha-sanshool alkaloids and polyphenol antioxidants with anti-inflammatory, circulation-stimulating, and free-radical-scavenging properties. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic extract concentrations and used in energizing and protective formulas for its bioactive alkaloid and antioxidant content.

Zea mays (corn) kernel extract provides zeaxanthin, lutein, and polyphenol antioxidants with free-radical-scavenging and photoprotective properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and antioxidant formulas for its carotenoid and polyphenol content.

Zea mays (corn) oil is a plant oil with a favorable linoleic acid (~55%) and oleic acid (~25%) balance providing emollient and barrier-supporting properties. It is well-tolerated by most skin types; its moderate oleic and saturated fatty acid content gives it some comedogenic potential at higher concentrations in acne-prone or congestion-prone skin.

Zea mays (corn) starch is used in cosmetics as a natural thickener, absorbent, and skin-feel modifier that provides a soft, silky, talc-like texture in powders and color cosmetics. It is considered well-tolerated, biodegradable, and used in natural-positioned formulas as an eco-friendly texturizer and oil absorbent.

This dual INCI/common name listing identifies the same ingredient — Zea Mays Starch (corn starch) — a natural thickener, absorbent, and skin-feel modifier that provides a soft, silky texture in powders and color cosmetics. It is well-tolerated, biodegradable, and used as an eco-friendly texturizer.

Zinc (listed generically) refers to elemental zinc or an unspecified zinc compound used for its documented antimicrobial activity against Cutibacterium acnes, sebum-regulating, and anti-inflammatory properties relevant to acne-prone and oily skin. The specific bioavailability and tolerability depend on the exact zinc salt or compound used in the formula.

The soft hyphen in '­Gluconate' is a data artifact from ingredient label parsing; this is zinc gluconate — a water-soluble zinc salt used in skincare as a sebum-regulating, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory active with documented efficacy against Cutibacterium acnes and inflammatory blemishes. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in blemish-targeting and soothing formulas for its multifunctional zinc activity.

Zinc oxide is a physical/mineral UV filter that scatters and reflects both UVA and UVB radiation without systemic absorption, providing the broadest single-filter sun protection spectrum available and the only UV filter approved by the FDA for both UVA and UVB protection. It is exceptionally well-tolerated — the preferred UV filter for sensitive, reactive, acne-prone, and post-procedure skin — though the standard particle size can leave a white cast that nano-sized variants partially address.

Zinc stearate is the zinc salt of stearic acid used in cosmetics as a mild astringent, slip enhancer, and anticaking agent in powders and color cosmetics, providing a smooth application feel and mild antimicrobial properties from its zinc content. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic concentrations.

Zingiber officinale (ginger) root extract provides gingerols, shogaols, and zingerone antioxidants with potent anti-inflammatory, circulation-stimulating, and free-radical-scavenging properties. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic extract concentrations and used in warming and protective formulas for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant content; it does not share the sensitization risk of ginger essential oil.
